description_long: This object is a tall, slender street light with a classic lantern-style lamp head mounted atop a straight cylindrical pole. The lamp head features a four-sided, tapered design with a pointed finial at the top, evoking a traditional Victorian or early 20th-century aesthetic. The lamp head appears to have a dark, opaque exterior, likely made of metal, with subtle paneling details and a slightly protruding cap. The pole is smooth and unadorned, extending vertically from a circular base that flares outward for stability. The base is simple and functional, designed to be anchored securely to the ground. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet elegant, suitable for urban streets, parks, or residential neighborhoods.
description: A tall, classic street light with a lantern-style lamp head and a smooth, unornamented pole mounted on a circular base. The design is traditional and functional, ideal for urban or park settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full height of the street light, with the lantern-style lamp head and straight pole clearly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, highlighting the symmetrical design and the pointed finial atop the lamp head.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the profile of the lamp head and the uniform cylindrical pole, with the base's circular shape evident.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the previous side, emphasizing the consistent, unadorned structure of the pole and lamp head.
attribute_lamp_head_design: Classic lantern-style with a four-sided, tapered body, pointed finial, and subtle paneling; no visible glass panes or ornate coverings.
